interpretation_summary: The replication attempt did not yield any analytical results.  The agent repeatedly rebuilt an R-based Docker image and tried to run the intended R script, but package-installation problems (lmerTest not found) and later corruption of replication_info.json led the orchestrator to point to a nonexistent Python entrypoint (“main.py”).  Because the code never executed, no coefficient, standard error, or p-value for the focal bilingualism variable were produced.  Therefore no substantive comparison with the original estimate (β = 2.68, p<.01) can be made.

fidelity_assessment.method_alignment: Low alignment: although the preregistration specified running the provided R mixed-effects model on the processed dataset, execution never reached model fitting.  Environment mismatches (missing R packages) and an erroneous switch to a Python entrypoint deviated substantially from the preregistered plan.

deviations.issue_description: Required R package lmerTest not installed in first build; subsequent Docker rebuilds attempted to fix, causing repeated failures.
deviations.impact: High: prevented lmer() model from running at all.

deviations.issue_description: replication_info.json became malformed, causing orchestrator to replace the R entrypoint with default main.py.
deviations.impact: High: container launched with an entry that does not exist, resulting in final execution halt.

results_comparison.hypothesis_tested: Bilingual students will score higher in English achievement than monolingual peers after controlling background covariates.
results_comparison.original_results: Positive coefficient for bilingualism (estimate = 2.68 WLE points; p < .01) controlling for cognitive, demographic and SES variables.
results_comparison.replication_results: No replication estimates available due to execution failure; model not run.
results_comparison.overall_answer: Unable to evaluate – replication produced no statistical output.

replication_report: Replication unsuccessful: execution errors prevented any empirical test of the bilingualism advantage claim.

failure_handling.suggestions: Ensure replication_info.json is valid JSON and retains the correct R entrypoint; re-generate Dockerfile only after confirming JSON validity.
Pre-install all required R packages (lme4, lmerTest, MuMIn, dplyr, tidyverse, reshape) in Dockerfile; verify script path and that readRDS uses full mounted path inside container.

notes: If code is fixed, also inspect logic for creating ‘bilingual’ dummy (ifelse condition seems wrong in script) and confirm weight handling; sensitivity analyses could then be executed.
